steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The plant absorbs and stores cadmium from the soil—soil cadmium concentrations decrease. These two clauses are connected as cause and effect, not as separate ideas or a contradiction.

steps.title: Select the transition showing consequence
steps.content:
“In turn” introduces the effect that results from the cause: the plant's absorption of cadmium causes the soil concentrations to drop.

The other choices mischaracterize the logical relationship: “specifically” frames the second clause as a specific instance of the first, “in addition” treats both as separate facts, and “nevertheless” signals contradiction.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
